steps.title: Spot the core action
steps.content: The passage shows authors helping to shape a distinctly Mexican literary tradition—a creative, formative process. The blank should express a parallel action of creation or actualization.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
The blank fills "the new nation ______ its autonomous identity." Test each choice:

A) overcame = defeated or conquered. "The nation overcame its autonomous identity" implies its identity was an obstacle to be defeated—contradicts the passage's positive, formative framing. Fails.

B) realized = made real, brought into existence. "The nation realized its autonomous identity" means it brought that identity into concrete form through literature. Matches the passage perfectly. Correct.

C) decried = publicly condemned. "The nation decried its autonomous identity" means it criticized its own identity—the opposite of what the passage describes. Fails.

D) evaded = escaped or avoided. "The nation evaded its autonomous identity" means it dodged or shunned its identity—contradicts the passage's formative, celebratory tone. Fails.
